paper.title: Search for Stellar Streams Based on Data from the RAVE5 and Gaia TGAS Catalogues

extraction.summary: No HVS candidates found. This paper searches for stellar streams in the solar neighborhood using proper motions from Gaia TGAS and line-of-sight velocities from RAVE5. The paper identifies three kinematic clumps (BB17-1, BB17-2, BB17-3) with large velocities (V<-150 km/s), associating BB17-3 with debris from the globular cluster ω Cen. The single mention of 'escape velocity from the Galaxy' (lines 248-250) is a passing technical note about a data quality cut (|Vr|<350 km/s) to filter out low-quality measurements with spuriously high velocities — it is not a claim about unbound or hypervelocity stars. The paper does not select for or discuss HVS, hypervelocity stars, or Galactic escape trajectories.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Three kinematic clumps (BB17-1, BB17-2, BB17-3) identified in the V vs sqrt(U^2+2V^2) velocity plane from >200,000 RAVE5+TGAS stars with V<-150 km/s. The paper searches for stellar streams, not HVS, and the velocity threshold is well below Galactic escape speed.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: No HVS candidates present. The paper identifies stellar streams (kinematic clumps) associated with known structures like ω Cen debris. The velocity range (V<-150 km/s) is far below the ~500+ km/s needed for unbound Galactic trajectories. The single mention of 'escape velocity from the Galaxy' (lines 248-250) refers to a data quality cut filtering out low-SNR measurements, not an HVS claim. Full paper text reviewed — zero claims of hypervelocity stars, unbound stars, or Galactic escape trajectories as a scientific finding.

source_refs.context: Abstract---We have analyzed the space velocities of stars with the proper motions and trigonometric parallaxes from the Gaia TGAS catalogue in combination with the line-of-sight velocities from the RAVE5 catalogue. In the V, sqrt(U^2+2V^2) velocity plane we have identified three clumps, BB17-1, BB17-2, and BB17-3, in the region of large velocities (V<-150 km/s). The stars of the BB17-1 and BB17-2 clumps are associated with the kinematic groups VelHel-6 and VelHel-7 detected previously by Helmi et al. We give the greatest attention to the BB17-3 clump. The latter is shown to be most closely linked with the debris of the globular cluster ω Cen.

More than 200 000 stars with the trigonometric parallaxes and proper motions from the Gaia TGAS catalogue and the line-of-sight velocities from the RAVE5 catalogue satisfy these constraints. The constraint on the magnitude of the line-of-sight velocity |Vr| is related to the existence of an appreciable number of low-quality measurements (with low signal-to noise ratios) with line-of-sight velocities exceeding the threshold escape velocity from the Galaxy.
